old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No pottery dependency remains because the catalogued mortar and pestle are made from travertine rather than ceramic material.

source_shape.source_locator: Artwork Details list the medium as travertine, also called Egyptian alabaster.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The directly catalogued pair is a stone artifact and supplies no evidence for ceramic manufacture or pottery use.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A separate vessel technology is neither a material component nor a necessary operating element of this use-neutral stone pair.

ontology_before: Pottery was attached to a mortar category even though the cited claim did not specify a ceramic artifact.
ontology_after: The stone artifact follows its catalogued material without an unrelated ceramic prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mortar_and_pestle no longer directly depends on pottery.
would_reject_if: The Met reclassified this mortar-and-pestle pair as ceramic or documented pottery as a necessary component of the object.
short_reason: The scoped pair is travertine, not pottery.
